resources.string: No readings yet. Configure your iSpindle to POST to the URL above.
Devices appear automatically after their first POST.
No devices yet — they show up after the first POST.
Calibration points are also removed. If the iSpindle posts again it will reappear as a new entry.
No SG data yet — add calibration points to compute SG from tilt.
SG: not enough trend yet — wait for more readings or widen the time window.
Lag phase · OG %1$s · current %2$s · %3$s h since first reading, no decline yet
Conditioning · OG %1$s · FG %2$s · %3$s%% ABV · yeast still cleaning up — verify stable readings before packaging
⚠ Stuck · OG %1$s · current %2$s · expected ~%3$s · flat for %4$s h · ABV so far %5$s%%
Cold crash · OG %1$s · FG (pre-crash) %2$s · apparent %3$s now · %4$s °C from %5$s °C · %6$s h in
At %1$s · SG %2$s · drop %3$s · %4$s%% ABV so far
Wait for more readings — the model needs at least 6 points spanning 2 h to fit. Check the iSpindle is reporting in.
Yeast is multiplying. Visible activity (krausen, airlock bubbles) usually starts within 12–48 h. If no SG drop after 72 h, check pitch viability and temperature.
Hold fermentation temperature steady. Don\'t open the fermenter — the krausen and CO₂ blanket protect the wort. The model\'s lag time λ is now identifiable; ETA refines as the descent continues.

Most of the attenuation has happened. Resist the urge to cold-crash before SG stabilises — yeast is still cleaning up flavour compounds. For lagers, raising temperature 2–3 °C now supports a diacetyl rest.

SG has reached the iSpindle\'s noise floor, but the ferment isn\'t biologically over. Yeast is reducing diacetyl/acetaldehyde and finishing slow sugars. Verify SG stable for 24–48 h before packaging. Cold-crash now if you want clarity.

Fermentation has plateaued above the expected FG. Common causes: temperature drop, low yeast viability, or unfermentable sugars. Try raising temperature 2–3 °C, gently rousing the yeast, or pitching a fresh starter.

Mid-ferment pause at SG %1$s for %2$s h, well above predicted FG. Most likely a diauxic shift — the yeast is finishing the easy sugars (glucose, maltose) before tackling the harder ones (maltotriose). Hold temperature steady; descent usually resumes within 24–48 h. If the SG hasn\'t resumed dropping after that, raise temperature 2–3 °C or gently rouse the yeast before assuming it\'s stuck.

Late-ferment pause at SG %1$s for %2$s h, close to predicted FG. SG is settling onto its asymptote rather than stalling — the yeast is on the harder sugars and starting to clean up flavour compounds (diacetyl, acetaldehyde). Give it 24–48 h; if SG holds, the state will switch to Conditioning. Resist cold-crashing until then.

Cold crash detected — temperature has dropped from %1$s °C toward %2$s °C. The apparent SG drop you\'re seeing is mostly density change (cooler beer reads denser, ~0.0001 SG per °C) plus CO₂ dissolution if you\'re carbonating, not further fermentation. The true FG is the SG you saw just before the crash started; that\'s what to record for ABV. Cold-crashing for 24–48 h flocculates yeast and clears the beer; raise temperature gently if you plan to reuse the yeast cake. To reuse: harvest some slurry into a sanitised jar, refrigerate; on brew day take the jar out, let it warm at room temp 2–3 hours, optionally make a small starter (1 L of 1.040 wort, oxygenate, hold at 20 °C for 12–24 h), then pitch.

Submerge the iSpindle in a sugar solution of known SG (e.g. pure water = 1.000, or a sugar/water mix measured with a refractometer). Wait for a reading, copy the tilt angle here, and enter the known SG.
Quadratic (degree 2) is the iSpindel community default. You need at least (degree+1) enabled points.
Fit failed — points may be collinear or duplicated.
Drives the iSpindle\'s config portal directly: joins its AP, scans your home networks, submits the form, and waits for the device to come online. Skips the manual browser step entirely.
MTB iSpindel PCB 4.0 runs the standard iSpindel firmware on an ESP8266. There is no Bluetooth — readings come in over WiFi as an HTTP POST.
Tap the IP to copy it.
On the Home tab, tap Start to bring up the HTTP server. A persistent notification confirms it is listening.
Boot the iSpindle into config mode: hold it horizontally for ~20 seconds at power-on. It raises an AP named \"iSpindel\".
Connect this phone to the \"iSpindel\" WiFi AP and open http://192.168.4.1/ in a browser to load the config portal.
In the portal: set WiFi credentials for your home network, set \"Service Type\" = HTTP, \"Server Address\" = %1$s, \"Server Port\" = %2$s, and a short sample interval (60 s works for testing).
Save. The iSpindle reboots, joins your home network, wakes, POSTs JSON, then sleeps. Reconnect this phone to the same home network.
Within one interval the Home tab shows the first reading. Open the Devices tab to see it listed, then Calibrate and Graph.
Calibration turns tilt angle into specific gravity. You need at least 3 reference solutions for a quadratic fit.
Pure water at 20 °C = SG 1.000. Let the iSpindle stabilise, read the angle from Home, and add it as (angle, 1.000) on the Calibrate tab.
Mix a sugar+water solution, measure SG with a refractometer or hydrometer, float the iSpindle, record (angle, SG).
Repeat for 2–3 more reference points across the expected SG range (1.000 → 1.080).
Tap \"Fit & save\". R² should be > 0.999 for a clean fit. Future readings auto-populate SG.
If the iSpindle is configured with a raw IP and the phone gets a different one after a DHCP renewal, the next reading is dropped. Two ways to keep the link alive across IP changes:
Reserve the phone\'s IP on your router. The setting is usually called \"DHCP reservation\", \"static lease\", or \"always assign the same IP\".

Use a hostname instead of an IP. Many home routers publish a DNS name for every DHCP client. On the Configure screen, tap \"Find hostname\" — if the router publishes a unique name for this phone you can use that as the iSpindle\'s server address, and it survives IP changes.

If \"Find hostname\" reports the name is ambiguous, the router has stale entries from previous devices. Give this phone a distinct name in the router\'s DHCP settings and remove the old entries, or fall back to a reserved IP.
Save device labels, calibration polynomials, and raw calibration points to a JSON file. Reading history isn\'t included — use the per-device CSV export for that.
• Phone and iSpindle must be on the same subnet. Check your router\'s guest-network isolation.
• If the phone\'s IP changes (DHCP lease expiry), re-enter the new IP in the iSpindle portal.
• The iSpindle deep-sleeps between intervals; you will not be able to ping it mid-cycle.
• For a static IP, reserve the phone\'s MAC on your router.
Approve the system dialog when it appears. The first time can take 10–20 s.
Programmatic WiFi join requires Android 10 (API 29) or newer.
Saving and waiting for iSpindle to join…
The device reboots and joins your home network. Poll runs every 1.5 s for up to 45 s.
Station IP: %1$s\n\nReconnect this phone to the same home WiFi to receive readings.
Save submitted, but join not confirmed

The iSpindle accepted the form but did not appear on the home network within the polling window. It may still join after a longer settle time. Reconnect this phone to home WiFi and check the Home tab.

1. Hold the iSpindle horizontally at power-on for ~20 s — it raises an AP named iSpindel_<chipID>.\n2. Tap Connect. If several iSpindles are in range Android shows a picker so you can choose the right one.\n3. Pick your home network, enter its password, save. The iSpindle reboots and joins.
Default firmware uses iSpindel_<chipID>; adjust only if you renamed it.
WiFi-scan permission was declined. Auto-pair can\'t proceed without it.
Pick the home network you want the iSpindle to join, then save.
Where the iSpindle should POST readings — defaults to this phone, which is usually what you want. Tap \"Use this phone\" to restore the defaults.
(no home IP detected — connect this phone to home WiFi first)
⚠ This app only receives readings when the iSpindle uses Generic HTTP (or HTTPS). Other services post to their own cloud and bypass the phone.
Use a router-published name so the iSpindle survives DHCP changes.
No reverse-DNS record for this phone — use the IP, or set a hostname in your router.
%1$s resolves to %2$d IPs — name is shared.
Other IPs answering to that name: %1$s
Imported. Will apply to this device on its first POST after pairing.
Couldn\'t parse this expression as a cubic in tilt — leaving the firmware copy in place. Re-enter calibration points by hand on the Calibrate tab if needed.
Writes the chosen polynomial into the iSpindle\'s POLYN field on Save & reboot. Useful so the firmware itself reports a sensible gravity even to other receivers, not just this app.
Pick a home network SSID first
